name: Debt Education & Certification
description_short: DECAF is a U.S. Trustee-approved provider of mandatory bankruptcy credit counseling (pre-filing) and debtor education courses (post-filing) required by federal law.
description_long:
Debt Education & Certification Foundation (DECAF) operates BKcert.com as an approved bankruptcy education provider. Founded in 2006 and based in Benbrook, Texas, the organization specializes exclusively in the two federally-mandated courses required in bankruptcy proceedings: pre-filing credit counseling and post-filing financial education.

DECAF offers both pre-bankruptcy and post-bankruptcy courses. Course 1 is the credit counseling requirement that must be completed before filing for bankruptcy under Chapter 7 or Chapter 13. Course 2 is the financial management/debtor education course required after filing and before debt discharge. Both courses are available online and via phone with 24/7 counselor support and technical assistance. The company explicitly serves both individual consumers and bankruptcy attorneys, offering no-cost attorney accounts for law firms to manage client requirements.

The company differentiates itself through several specific features: U.S. Trustee approval status, couples can complete courses together at no additional cost, unbiased counseling positioning, and comprehensive availability (online, phone, and live counselors available around-the-clock). They advertise expertise in facilitating attorney-client needs and emphasize convenience through multiple delivery methods. Fee waivers appear to be available for those with qualifying circumstances.

As a specialized bankruptcy education provider, DECAF's scope is narrowly focused and non-discretionary—their services exist to meet legal requirements rather than to offer optional financial improvement tools. While approval by the U.S. Trustee indicates regulatory compliance, the disclaimer that "approval does not endorse or assure the quality of a provider's services" is prominently displayed. The company's value proposition centers on convenience and accessibility rather than transformative debt reduction.
pros: U.S. Trustee approved agency for bankruptcy education courses
Couples can complete both courses together at no additional charge
24/7 availability including online courses, phone courses, and live counselor support
No-cost, no-obligation attorney account setup for law firms and bankruptcy lawyers
Both pre-filing (Course 1) and post-filing (Course 2) courses available through single provider
Fee waivers available for those meeting qualifications
Long operational history since 2006 with established infrastructure
cons: Narrowly specialized service—only useful if actively filing for bankruptcy, not for general credit improvement
U.S. Trustee approval disclaimer explicitly states approval does not guarantee quality of services
Pricing information not displayed on website; no transparency on course costs unless fee waiver-eligible
Limited to bankruptcy-specific education; does not address broader debt management or credit repair
Company operates in regulated compliance space with limited differentiation between competitors
best_for: Individuals preparing to file for Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy
Bankruptcy attorneys seeking to comply with client education requirements
Married couples filing jointly seeking to complete courses together cost-efficiently
Consumers with fee waiver qualifications seeking free mandated bankruptcy counseling
diagnosis: DECAF is essential for anyone actively filing for bankruptcy, as their courses are federally mandated by the U.S. Trustee and non-negotiable prerequisites. The main caveat is that this service only applies to bankruptcy filers—it is not a discretionary debt solution or credit repair tool, and the company explicitly does not provide credit repair, debt settlement, or general financial counseling beyond bankruptcy-specific education requirements.
services: Pre-filing credit counseling course (Course 1) - required before bankruptcy filing
Post-filing debtor education/financial management course (Course 2) - required after filing
Joint couple course completion (no additional charge)
Consumer login and course tracking portal
meta_description: DECAF, U.S. Trustee-approved provider of mandatory bankruptcy credit counseling and debtor education courses required by federal law.
